titleStmt.title: Letter from Daulat Ram Kalia, 10 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Daulat Ram Kalia

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from Indian student Daulat Ram Kalia to Dublin Castle. The letter was written in the hope that it would be passed on to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Churchill Guest, Lord Wimborne (1873-1939). Kalia writes to Wimborne to thank him for the action taken and the sympathy displayed to the Indian students. Kalia had previously written to complain about the newspaper 'The Eye-Opener'. The paper caused much controversy in 1916 when it printed an article with the heading 'Black Peril' which attacked Indian students and indeed all people of dark skin. The paper not only claimed that 'coloured people' were savage but also that any white person seen in their company was a disgrace. Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/6388/1

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dear sir I have to acknowledge the
receipt of your letter No. 5575 dated 6.4.15
with many thanks. I do not know how to thank
His Excellency for the sympathy he has shown
with the Indian students & for the
action he has so kindly taken. The Indian students with whom I
have met have expressed their gratefu
-lness to Lord Wimborne & highly ap
-preciated his kindness. This step of His Excellency will
have a far reaching effect is as much
as these students (who or at least some
of whom are to be the leaders of the Indian
public opinion in time to come)
will remember this favour & kindness of His Excellency throughout their lives.
Some small events sometimes produce
very great & far reaching results & the present one is
surely one of them. May I ask you to kindly convey my heartfelt
thanks to His Excellency for all this. I have also to thank yourself so much
for the kindly interest you have taken
in the matter & assure you that the
Indians never forget kindness shown to
them & they have the virtue of being
grateful. Thanking you once more Yours Very Obediently D. R. Kalia
